overview.headline: A focused, fixed-scope assessment of the Skyworks ODS environment.
overview.background: The ODS serves dual duty as a transactional and analytical layer, creating performance constraints at peak concurrency, limiting governance visibility, and introducing cost inefficiencies. BPC performs deep metadata ingestion, references upstream and downstream systems for lineage, classifies datasets into a Bronze/Silver/Gold medallion architecture, and produces a prioritized, low-risk transition backlog.
overview.outcome: All findings, lineage maps, classification reports, and the transition backlog are delivered as final outputs owned by Skyworks.

milestones.name: Baseline Delivery
milestones.trigger: Lineage map and classification report accepted

layers.name: Collection
layers.summary: Discovery and metadata ingestion across the ODS: domain intelligence, an automated catalog, enrichment, and transformation analysis.

subparts.name: Domain Intelligence
subparts.desc: Business domain context and data ownership captured with SMEs.

intel.title: BPC inference
intel.note: Inferred by BPC from the D2 metadata inventory - schema names, object composition, and row-count profiling.

items.text: This is a semiconductor manufacturer's operational core: the estate spans SAP ECC (LIB_ECC_RTP) alongside the Skyworks ODS and warehouse, so the analytical value sits in sales and demand planning, not transactional capture.

Demand planning is the high-value domain - the largest tables are sd.FactVersions (1.09B rows), FactSalesAndDemandPlanning, and sap.JCDS / sap.JEST document tables, marking planning and SAP status objects as the analytical hub.
Skyworks_ODS is the anchor: 71,664 columns across 2,773 tables and 9.09B rows - the central hub every lineage path traces back to.
The estate carries a large dormant tail: 558 empty tables and 2,891 of 5,108 programmable objects untouched since before 2024 - deprecation candidates for the transition backlog.

subparts.name: Automated Data Catalog
subparts.desc: Automated ingestion of ODS tables, schemas, columns and sizes.

subparts.name: Data Enrichment
subparts.desc: Usage patterns, access tools, and frequency from query-log analysis.

intel.title: BPC data annotation
intel.note: Business meaning BPC attached to the estate, with the evidence from the D2 inventory it used to decide.

items.note: Largest fact in the estate (1.09B rows) - versioned demand-planning grain.
items.why: Top demand-planning table, actively maintained, anchors the sales/planning analytical core the assessment prioritizes.

items.note: SAP document status change tables (1.08B / 1.05B rows).
items.why: Upstream ECC status objects - lineage roots for order and document flows; conform to Silver, do not deprecate.

items.note: Empty tables carrying schema but no data.
items.why: 0 rows and no downstream reads - low-risk deprecation candidates to weigh in the transition backlog.

items.note: 78% of all 164,543 columns are loosely constrained.
items.why: High nullability lowers confidence in key integrity - a signal that weighs against premature Gold classification.

subparts.name: Transformation
subparts.desc: SSIS pipeline parsing and ETL / database assessment of transformation logic.

meaning.title: What does the data mean?
meaning.subtitle: BPC reads the D2 catalog and answers in business terms - not just what the databases are, but what they say about the Skyworks data estate.
meaning.author: BPC · Business Process Co-Pilot

questions.q: What business is this data describing?
questions.a: A semiconductor manufacturer's operational data store. 18 databases carry the sales, demand-planning and SAP document flows that run the business - 5,471 tables, 164,543 columns, and 28.5 billion rows in all.

In four estates. Skyworks_ODS, Skyworks_ODS_Archive, LIB_ECC_RTP and Skyworks_WH together hold over 99% of the 28.5B rows, and the largest tables are demand-planning and SAP fact tables - the high-value analytical core to classify and conform first.

questions.q: What does the data warn us about?
questions.a: Dormancy and loose constraints. 558 tables are empty and 2,891 programmable objects are untouched since before 2024 - a deprecation tail; and 78% of columns are nullable, a data-quality signal that must weigh in the Bronze/Silver/Gold classification.

layers.name: Unification
layers.summary: End-to-end lineage across ODS datasets and the nine reference systems, with data ownership mapping and manual additions where automation is limited.

subparts.name: End-to-End Visualization
subparts.desc: Lineage from upstream sources through transformations to downstream consumption.

subparts.name: Manual Additions
subparts.desc: Analyst-added lineage where API or metadata access is limited.

topSignal.title: Signal that matters most
topSignal.subtitle: As lineage mapping (D3) proceeds, BPC ranks which signal to trace first across the 9 reference systems for the classification decision.
topSignal.author: BPC · Business Process Co-Pilot

primary.why: The number of certified consumers hanging off a dataset predicts Gold candidacy better than row count. The demand-planning facts (sd.FactVersions) and the SAP status roots fan out the widest, so their lineage is worth resolving first.

ranked.why: LIB_ECC_RTP feeds the ODS - broken or ambiguous keys here corrupt every downstream mart, so key integrity across this boundary is the gating lineage risk.
An unowned dataset can't be promoted or governed; ownership mapping hasn't started, so every Gold candidate is currently unassigned.
9 reference systems feed many marts - stale part / customer master ripples widely but changes slowly.

layers.name: Presentation
layers.summary: The face of the assessment: classification, alignment, simulation, backlog, playbook, and executive summary, packaged for Skyworks to act and own.

subparts.name: Classification & Alignment
subparts.desc: Bronze/Silver/Gold classification and current-state alignment baseline.

subparts.name: Impact & Backlog
subparts.desc: Cost/performance simulation and the prioritized transition backlog.

subparts.name: Enablement
subparts.desc: Transition playbook, walkthroughs, and executive summary.

deliverables.name: Connectivity Status Report
deliverables.purpose: Validation of ODS metadata access and lineage reference availability

deliverables.name: ODS Metadata Inventory
deliverables.purpose: Complete catalog of ODS datasets, tables, schemas, columns, and SSIS definitions
deliverables.delayReason: Delayed to rerun results on Production on client request. Production access pending.

deliverables.name: End-to-End ODS Lineage Map
deliverables.purpose: Visual, data-backed lineage from upstream sources to downstream consumption

deliverables.name: Architecture Alignment Baseline
deliverables.purpose: Current-state alignment percentage to target medallion architecture with gap analysis

deliverables.name: Cost & Performance Simulation Model
deliverables.purpose: Interactive model of projected latency and compute impact for recommended transitions

deliverables.name: Prioritized Transition Backlog
deliverables.purpose: Ranked backlog of dataset transitions with risk, effort, and impact

deliverables.name: Transition Playbook
deliverables.purpose: Step-by-step guidance for Skyworks teams to execute transitions incrementally

deliverables.name: Executive Summary Report
deliverables.purpose: Findings, recommendations, and business case summary for leadership

bpcProcessing.title: How BPC processed the Skyworks estate
bpcProcessing.subtitle: The Business Process Co-Pilot runs every asset through the same pipeline. This is where the Skyworks ODS engagement stands.
bpcProcessing.author: BPC · Business Process Co-Pilot

stages.name: Ingest
stages.detail: Read schemas, columns, and row counts across every in-scope database.

stages.name: Infer
stages.detail: Derived the business model and the four high-value estates from the catalog.

stages.name: Annotate
stages.detail: Tagged demand-planning facts, SAP roots, dormant tables, and nullability.

stages.name: Classify
stages.detail: Lineage (D3) proceeding; Bronze / Silver / Gold classification starts W4.

stages.name: Recommend
stages.detail: Cost/performance simulation, transition backlog, playbook, and exec summary.

confidence.label: Model confidence on this estate
confidence.detail: Weighted across inference and annotation passes on the D2 inventory.
